items.headline: Council considers 9 new homes plus accessory units on Old Trace Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking approval to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot into nine residential lots, enabling construction of nine single-family homes and seven junior accessory dwelling units (JADUs) in the Residential Estate zone. This is a quasi-judicial hearing where the council will decide whether to recommend approval of the tentative map.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment project advances at El Camino Real
items.summary: Palo Alto Greens HOA approved tree replacement as part of the larger Summerhill redevelopment at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant mixed-use project in one of Palo Alto's key commercial corridors.

items.headline: Mountain View approves financing for new public safety building
items.summary: The City Council approved lease revenue bonds to fund a new public safety building project. This represents a significant capital investment in police and emergency services infrastructure.

items.headline: City council reviews biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: The council held a study session on a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan, which will guide how Mountain View manages trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat across the city. This policy could affect landscaping requirements, park development, and environmental protections.

items.headline: City discusses future management of middle school athletics program
items.summary: The council heard a 45-minute staff presentation on the direction and management model for Palo Alto's middle school athletics program, suggesting the city may be reconsidering how it operates or funds this youth program.

items.headline: City releases planning and economic strategy progress reports
items.summary: The City Council received semi-annual updates on citywide planning activities, major events, and its economic strategy work plan. These reports show the city's current development priorities and progress on strategic initiatives affecting residents.

items.headline: Sunnyvale plans 67-unit mixed-use development with retail
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a proposed project on 4.22 acres that would add 55 townhomes, 12 apartment flats, and approximately 18,500 square feet of retail space including a new two-story building while keeping the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The development would be subdivided into 17 residential lots plus common areas, representing a significant residential expansion in the city.

items.headline: Campbell raises business license tax rates for fiscal year
items.summary: The city council amended the municipal code to increase business license tax rates effective in the new fiscal year. This annual update affects local businesses operating in Campbell and may have indirect effects on consumers through potential price adjustments.
